questions.question:
Consider the following table named Student in a relational database. The primary key of this table is rollNum.

Student
rollNum | name  | gender | marks
--------+-------+--------+------
1       | Naman | M      | 62
2       | Aliya | F      | 70
3       | Aliya | F      | 74
4       | Janum | M      | 82
5       | Swati | F      | 65

The SQL query below is executed on this database.

SELECT *
FROM Student
WHERE gender = 'F'
AND marks > 65;

The number of rows returned by the query is ___________.
questions.to_find: Number of rows returned by the SQL query with conditions gender = 'F' AND marks > 65

understand.plain: We have a Student table with 5 rows. The query filters rows where gender is 'F' (female) AND marks is strictly greater than 65. We need to count how many rows satisfy both conditions simultaneously.

keywords.explain: Both conditions must be true for a row to be included in the result. gender = 'F' filters only female students, and marks > 65 keeps only those with marks strictly above 65 (not equal to 65).
keywords.example: Row with marks = 65 does NOT satisfy marks > 65, so it is excluded.

keywords.explain: The operator '>' means strictly greater than, excluding the boundary value. So marks > 65 means marks must be 66 or higher.
keywords.example: Swati has marks = 65, which does not satisfy marks > 65, so she is excluded.

understand.visual_alt: Student table with 5 rows evaluated against the WHERE conditions. Rows 2 and 3 (Aliya with marks 70 and 74) are highlighted green as they satisfy both gender='F' and marks>65. Rows 1 and 4 fail because gender='M'. Row 5 (Swati) fails because marks=65 is not strictly greater than 65.

given.aim: Find the number of rows in the Student table where gender = 'F' AND marks > 65

terms.meaning: Condition that keeps only female students
terms.example: Rows 2, 3, 5 have gender='F'; rows 1, 4 have gender='M'
terms.connects: Reduces candidates from 5 rows to 3 rows (Aliya-70, Aliya-74, Swati-65)

terms.meaning: Strict inequality; marks must be strictly more than 65 (boundary 65 is excluded)
terms.example: Swati has marks=65, so 65 > 65 is FALSE; she is excluded
terms.connects: From the 3 female rows, only marks=70 and marks=74 satisfy this condition

terms.meaning: Both conditions must simultaneously be true for a row to appear in the result
terms.example: Janum has marks=82 but gender='M', so AND fails for Janum
terms.connects: Combining both filters, exactly 2 rows pass: Aliya(70) and Aliya(74)

given.plan: Step through each row and check both conditions. Count the rows where gender is 'F' AND marks is strictly greater than 65.

steps.title: Apply gender = 'F' filter
steps.apply:
Row 1: Naman, M, 62 — FAIL (gender='M')
Row 2: Aliya, F, 70 — PASS
Row 3: Aliya, F, 74 — PASS
Row 4: Janum, M, 82 — FAIL (gender='M')
Row 5: Swati, F, 65 — PASS
Rows passing first filter: {2, 3, 5}
steps.note: 3 female students remain after this filter.

steps.title: Apply marks > 65 filter to remaining rows
steps.apply:
Row 2: Aliya, F, 70 — 70 > 65? YES — INCLUDED
Row 3: Aliya, F, 74 — 74 > 65? YES — INCLUDED
Row 5: Swati, F, 65 — 65 > 65? NO (equal, not greater) — EXCLUDED
Rows in final result: {2, 3}
steps.note: Critically, marks = 65 does NOT satisfy marks > 65. Only strict inequality qualifies.

solution.result: 2 rows are returned by the query.

questions.formula_note: SQL WHERE clause with AND operator: both conditions must be satisfied; strict inequality (>) excludes the boundary value.

questions.question:
Consider the following database tables of a sports league.

player (pid, pname, age)
team (tid, tname, city, cid)
coach (cid, cname)
members (pid, tid)

Instances of the tables:

player:
pid | pname    | age
----+----------+----
1   | Jasprit  | 31
2   | Atharva  | 24
3   | Ilhan    | 26
4   | Axar     | 30

coach:
cid | cname
----+------
101 | Ricky
102 | Mark
103 | Trevor

team:
tid | tname | city   | cid
----+-------+--------+----
10  | MI    | Mumbai | 102
20  | DC    | Delhi  | 101
30  | PK    | Mohali | 103

members:
pid | tid
----+----
1   | 10
2   | 30
3   | 10
4   | 20

The following SQL query is executed on this database:

SELECT MIN(P.age)
FROM player P, members M, team T, coach C
WHERE P.pid = M.pid
AND M.tid = T.tid
AND T.cid = C.cid
AND C.cname = 'Mark';

The value returned by the given SQL query is _______ (Answer in integer).
questions.to_find: The minimum age of players who play in the team coached by Mark

understand.plain: We have four related tables for a sports league. The SQL query joins all four tables to find players who are in a team coached by 'Mark', and then returns the minimum (youngest) age among those players.

keywords.explain: The query links player to members (via pid), members to team (via tid), and team to coach (via cid) to trace which players belong to which team and who coaches that team.
keywords.example: player P join members M on P.pid=M.pid gives us (Jasprit,10), (Atharva,30), (Ilhan,10), (Axar,20)

keywords.explain: Returns the smallest value in a set. MIN(P.age) returns the age of the youngest qualifying player.
keywords.example: MIN(31, 26) = 26 — Ilhan is younger than Jasprit among MI players.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='520' height='260'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='520' height='260' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)'/><text x='260' y='22' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Chain: Mark -> MI (tid=10) -> Players in MI</text><rect x='20' y='35' width='120' height='40' class='hi'/><text x='80' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Coach Mark</text><text x='80' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>cid=102</text><rect x='190' y='35' width='120' height='40' class='hi'/><text x='250' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Team MI</text><text x='250' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>tid=10, cid=102</text><rect x='360' y='10' width='140' height='40' class='ok' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='430' y='27' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Jasprit (pid=1)</text><text x='430' y='43' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>age = 31</text><rect x='360' y='60' width='140' height='40' class='ok' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='430' y='77' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Ilhan (pid=3)</text><text x='430' y='93' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>age = 26</text><line x1='140' y1='55' x2='190' y2='55' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='310' y1='45' x2='360' y2='30' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='310' y1='55' x2='360' y2='80' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><text x='80' y='120' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>cname=Mark</text><text x='250' y='120' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>city=Mumbai</text><rect x='130' y='140' width='260' height='50' class='hi'/><text x='260' y='162' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>MIN(age) = MIN(31, 26)</text><text x='260' y='182' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>= 26  (Ilhan is youngest)</text><rect x='160' y='205' width='200' height='40' class='ok' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='260' y='230' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>Answer = 26</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Diagram showing the join chain: Coach Mark (cid=102) connects to team MI (tid=10, cid=102), which connects to players in members table with tid=10: Jasprit (pid=1, age=31) and Ilhan (pid=3, age=26). MIN(age) = 26.

given.aim: Find the minimum age of players whose team is coached by the coach named 'Mark'

terms.meaning: Filter condition to identify the coach named Mark
terms.example: From coach table: Mark has cid=102
terms.connects: Starting point — identifies cid=102 to find which team Mark coaches

terms.meaning: Links the team table to the coach table via the cid foreign key
terms.example: Team MI has cid=102, which matches Mark's cid=102, so MI is Mark's team (tid=10)
terms.connects: Identifies tid=10 (MI, Mumbai) as the team coached by Mark

terms.term: M.tid = T.tid and P.pid = M.pid (members join)
terms.meaning: Links players to their teams through the members junction table
terms.example: members rows (1,10) and (3,10) mean pid=1 (Jasprit) and pid=3 (Ilhan) play for tid=10 (MI)
terms.connects: Identifies Jasprit (age=31) and Ilhan (age=26) as players in Mark's team

terms.meaning: Aggregate function that returns the smallest age value among qualifying rows

given.plan: Trace the join chain: find coach Mark's cid (102), find the team with cid=102 (MI, tid=10), find all members with tid=10 (pid=1 and pid=3), look up their ages (31 and 26), and return the minimum age.

steps.title: Identify coach Mark and his team
steps.apply:
From coach table: Mark has cid = 102.
From team table: Team with cid = 102 is MI (tid=10, city=Mumbai).
So Mark coaches team MI with tid = 10.
steps.note: The join T.cid = C.cid links coach to team via the cid foreign key.

steps.title: Find players in team MI (tid=10)
steps.apply:
From members table, rows with tid=10:
  (pid=1, tid=10) -> Jasprit (age=31)
  (pid=3, tid=10) -> Ilhan (age=26)
Note: Atharva (pid=2) is in tid=30 (PK), Axar (pid=4) is in tid=20 (DC) — both excluded.
steps.note: Two players play for MI: Jasprit and Ilhan.

steps.title: Compute MIN(age) for qualifying players
steps.apply:
Ages of players in Mark's team: Jasprit = 31, Ilhan = 26.
MIN(31, 26) = 26.
steps.note: Ilhan (age 26) is the youngest player in the team coached by Mark.

questions.formula_note: Multi-table JOIN with aggregate MIN(): trace foreign key chains (coach->team via cid, team->members via tid, members->player via pid) and apply MIN() on the filtered result set.
